title: JSON-LD + Turtle + audio-bundle export tooling
context: Downstream consumers (TTS developers, accessibility tools, classrooms, Wikimedia projects) need the corpus in standard open-linked-data formats. The export tooling must produce JSON-LD and Turtle representations of the corpus using the persistent IDs from place-name-pronunciation-id-001, link Place records to their Wikidata QIDs and GeoNames IDs, include attribution strings, and bundle per-entry audio artifacts. Exports must validate and round-trip under the persistent-ID namespace. Depends on place-name-pronunciation-recon-001.
objective: Implement TypeScript/ESM export tooling that generates: JSON-LD export of the full corpus with Wikidata/GeoNames links and proper attribution; Turtle/RDF export of the same; per-entry audio bundles (archival + derived formats + checksums); and a CSV/JSON data package. All exports must use the persistent ID namespace and include license and attribution metadata per entry.
acceptanceCriteria: JSON-LD export is produced and validates against the JSON-LD specification; Place IRIs use the persistent namespace from place-name-pronunciation-id-001.
Turtle/RDF export is produced and parses cleanly with a standard RDF parser.
Each exported Place links to its Wikidata QID and GeoNames ID via standard linked-data predicates (schema:sameAs or equivalent).
Per-entry audio bundles include archival + derived audio files and SHA-256 checksums.
License and attribution metadata is included per entry in all export formats.
Exports round-trip: importing the JSON-LD back produces records equivalent to the canonical JSON source.
Export tooling is wired into pnpm build/test/lint and CI passes.
Maintainer reviews and merges the PR.
output: Export tooling package (TypeScript/ESM) committed via PR, generating JSON-LD, Turtle, and audio bundles from the corpus with persistent IDs, attribution, and passing CI.
